feedback: Strong post. The personal billing comparison ($8K vs Sora's 12-minute burn) is the kind of specific, lived detail that earns attention. Voice is consistent throughout — builder's perspective, not commentator's. Closing question is genuine and earns a response.

dimension_notes.voice: pass — multiple first-person references, fragments present ('Two failures.', 'Sora was never that.'), natural sentence rhythm, no banned words or phrases
dimension_notes.framing: pass — single reaction to a specific news moment, personal moment (checking own billing) makes it feel lived-in not researched
dimension_notes.platform: pass — ~1487 chars in sweet spot, hook lands in first 159 chars, closing question is specific and earns engagement, URL present
dimension_notes.epistemic: pass — confident take with one genuine hedge ('Maybe they never really asked it'), calibrated to the strength of the claim
dimension_notes.structure: pass — strong burstiness, paragraphs vary in length and density, no neat-bow closing
dimension_notes.framework: pass — nut-graf structure: facts open (numbers), personal anchor, analysis, opinion, question; evidence grammar holds
